Notice And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Civil Proceedings; Appendix Of Non-California Authorities
Off Calendar Jul-08-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT URS CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY CIVIL PROCEEDINGS. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Grant. Arbitration agreement is enforceable. It is neither substantively nor procedurally unconscionable. Defendant has not waived its rights to arbitration. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/MJM) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Court Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153

MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-01-2010 Unknown
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF HEIDI NELKIE MOTION TO STRIKE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES IN DEFENDANT'S FIRST AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is denied. Request for sanctions denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MOTION To Quash Or Modify Defendant'S Subpoena To Engineering/Remediation Resources Group, Inc.
Off Calendar Apr-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
RORY C. QUINTANA, ESQ. (KUMIN SOMMERS LLP), (415)434-4500, FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY, HEIDI NELKIE
ASHLEY E. CHOREN, ESQ., (415)397-2823, FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY

HEARING WAS HAD. THEREAFTER, tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF HEIDI NELKIE'S Motion To Quash Or Modify Defendant's Subpoena To Engineering/Remediation Resources Group, Inc. is granted as the subpoena is overly broad. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported. (610/EAH)

DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-08-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF HEIDI NELKIE DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Rory C. Quintana, Esq. of Kumin Sommers LLP (415) 434-4500 appearing for Plaintiff and moving party. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer overruled as to 1st and 2nd affirmative defenses. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to remaining defenses for defendant to plead them with particularity. Order signed in open Court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
